thinking.text:
The search results don't specifically mention Zoom Video as an exclusion. Let me check the transaction data again for the Zoom Video transaction:

txn_a8f1c2d3e411 - Zoom Video - $149.99 - 09/15/2025 - Software - 600 points (Business Silver Rewards Card)

$149.99 × 10% = 1499.9 points, but only 600 points were earned.
600 / 149.99 = 4% 

So Zoom earned 4% instead of 10%. This is strange because Zoom is a SaaS platform for videoconferencing, which should qualify for the 10% rate according to the qualifying categories.

Wait, let me check the Business Bronze Rewards Card exclusions I found earlier:

From doc_business_credit_cards_business_bronze_rewards_card_003:
"SaaS Subscriptions (after first 12 months only):
The following SaaS merchants earn the 1.0% rate for the first 12 months of your subscription only. After the initial 12-month period, auto-renewals and continued subscription payments to these merchants earn 0% cash back:
- Slack
- Zoom
- HubSpot
- Salesforce"

This is for the Business Bronze Rewards Card, not the Business Silver Rewards Card. But it's possible there are similar restrictions for the Business Silver Rewards Card.

Actually, looking at the transaction again:
- Zoom Video - $149.99 - 09/15/2025 - Software - 600 points

600 points on $149.99 = 4% (not 1% or 10%)

This is strange. Let me think about this...

Actually, wait. The user's Business Silver Rewards Card was opened on 02/13/2025. The double cash back promo runs for the first 6 months after account opening. So the promo would end around 08/13/2025.


The Zoom transaction on 09/15/2025 falls after the promotional period ended, so it should earn the standard 10% rate for software. But it only earned 4%, which suggests either Zoom has specific exclusions on this card or there's a merchant coding issue. I should check if there are any documented exclusions for the Business Silver Rewards Card similar to what I found for the Bronze tier.

tool_calls.Name: KB_search
tool_calls.Arguments: {"query": "Business Silver Rewards Card Zoom exclusion SaaS subscription restriction"}
